skill.name: readme-i18n
skill.description: Translate repository README files into localized Markdown variants while preserving links, badges, code examples, anchors, and language selectors.
skill.summary: Localize README files and keep GitHub Markdown mechanics working.
skill.author: xixu-me

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many backtick-delimited Markdown examples as Ruby or shell execution, but the reviewed files contain documentation instructions and Markdown samples, not runnable scripts. The API key text is an example environment variable that the skill tells users not to translate, and no prompt-injection attempt or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread As Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited locations are Markdown inline code, code fences, example filenames, selector markers, links, or README syntax guidance. These are inert documentation examples and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations were manually reviewed and are Markdown examples or inline code spans. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command invocation is present in those files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Example Secret Name Is Preservation Guidance
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported environment access at SKILL.md line 84, but that line only names OPENAI_API_KEY as an example environment variable that should remain untranslated. The skill does not read environment variables or transmit secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is inside a do-not-translate list for README literal tokens. There is no code path that accesses process environment values.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Terms Are Markdown Tokens
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are triggered by short Markdown tokens such as md, http, backticks, and anchor syntax in documentation checklists. They describe README preservation checks, not cryptographic operations or host or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these lines are README localization guidance and verification checklist text. No hashing, encryption, host probing, or network scanning behavior is present.

content.value_statement: README translations often break badges, links, anchors, and code examples. This skill translates the prose layer while preserving repository mechanics and adding consistent language selectors.
actual_capabilities: Creates localized README sibling files using repository naming patterns.
Adds or updates a shared language selector across README variants.
Preserves Markdown structure, code fences, badges, URLs, and file paths.
Translates human-language prose while leaving commands and environment variables unchanged.
Rewrites same-file anchor links when localized headings change.
Applies glossary and do-not-translate terms consistently.
limitations: It focuses on README files, not full application or website internationalization.
It may ask for clarification when target languages are unclear.
Specialized technical terms still benefit from human review.
It cannot verify external link availability beyond README link structure.

use_cases.title: Add First README Translation
use_cases.description: Create a new localized README and add a language selector without changing badges, commands, or package names.

use_cases.title: Expand Existing Localized Docs
use_cases.description: Add another language variant while preserving the repository's existing filename pattern and selector style.

use_cases.title: Refresh Translated README Files
use_cases.description: Update localized README variants after the source README changes, while keeping anchors and selectors aligned.

prompt_templates.title: Translate One README
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single target language.
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate README.md into Spanish. Create README.es.md and keep code blocks, commands, links, and badges unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Add Multiple Languages
prompt_templates.scenario: Create several localized siblings and wire navigation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Make this repository multilingual. Add Chinese and French README variants, then add a language selector to every README file.

prompt_templates.title: Follow Existing Conventions
prompt_templates.scenario: Work in a repository that already has localized files.
prompt_templates.prompt: We already have README.zh.md. Add README.es.md and update the existing language selector in place without creating a second selector.

prompt_templates.title: Use Glossary And Verify Anchors
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced localization with terminology rules and final checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Localize the README into Japanese using the provided glossary. Preserve product names, update same-file anchors, and report any terms left untranslated.

output_examples.input: Translate README.md into Spanish and add a language selector.
output_examples.output: Creates README.es.md, updates README.md with a selector, preserves code examples, and reports assumptions about terminology.

output_examples.input: Add Chinese to a repository that already has English and Spanish README files.
output_examples.output: Creates the Chinese README, updates all selector blocks, keeps language order consistent, and verifies internal README links.

output_examples.input: Refresh localized README files after the English README changed.
output_examples.output: Updates changed prose in each localized file, keeps unchanged examples intact, and checks that anchors still resolve.

best_practices: Provide target languages and any glossary terms before starting.
Review localized headings when anchor links are important.
Keep README.md as the source of truth unless the repository uses another convention.
anti_patterns: Do not translate commands, package names, environment variables, or file paths.
Do not add a second language selector when one already exists.
Do not reorder sections unless the user explicitly requests a documentation restructure.

faq.question: Does this skill translate code examples?
faq.answer: No. It preserves code fences, inline code, commands, flags, and configuration examples.

faq.question: What file names does it use?
faq.answer: It defaults to README language suffixes, such as README.es.md, unless the repository already uses another pattern.

faq.question: Can it add a language switcher?
faq.answer: Yes. It can add or update a single selector block across all README variants.

faq.question: Does it support glossaries?
faq.answer: Yes. It can apply glossary terms and do-not-translate lists across every target language.

faq.question: Will it update internal anchor links?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks same-file anchor links and rewrites them when translated headings change.

faq.question: Is it for full product localization?
faq.answer: No. It is focused on README documentation, not application strings, websites, or complete localization systems.
